title: repeat
text: repeat Define a repeat / until loop. repeat ... one or more ProcScript instructions ... until ( Condition ) Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Condition Boolean Boolean expression that must evaluate to TRUE before the repeat loop can end. Uniface converts any data types specified in the expression to boolean data types. For more information, see Type Conversion . Return Values The repeat statement does not affect $status . Use Allowed in all component types. Description The repeat statement loops through the ProcScript statements that follow it until the Condition on the until clause is evaluated as TRUE (that is, nonzero). Uniface then leaves the loop and continues with the statement following the until clause (if any). Use the break statement to terminate a repeat / until loop early. Conditional statements such as if / endif , while / endwhile , and repeat / until can be nested up to 32 levels. It can be useful to indent all statements between the repeat and corresponding until . Example: Repeat/Until Loop The following example shows the use of the repeat statement. The example loops through all the occurrences of entity E1, until it finds an occurrence where the values of the fields F2 and NAMEDUMMY are the same, or until all the occurrences have been checked. ; F2.E1 is a character string field containing a name ; loop until field matches or setocc is out of occurrences ; field: NAMEDUMMY.E1 if (F2.E1 != NAMEDUMMY) vCurrOcc = $curocc $NAME$ = NAMEDUMMY i = 1 repeat setocc "E1", i i = (i + 1) until ((f2.E1 = $NAME$) | ($status < 0)) if ($status < 0) message "%%$NAME$%%% is not available." setocc "E1", vCurrOcc return (-1) endif endif $prompt = F2.E1 Copy Related Links break Unconditionally exit a loop initiated by for, forentity, forlist, repeat, or while. selectcase Define a block of conditional case selections. Conditions A condition is a logical expression that evaluates to a boolean value. It is typically used in conditional processing initiated by ProcScript statements such as if, repeat, selectcase, and while. Operators Operators perform arithmetic, relational, logical, string manipulation, struct member de-reference, operation de-reference, and field indirection operations in ProcScript instructions.
